nodes.title: Cursor Deep Dive
nodes.description: Go beyond the basics — master Chat, Inline Edit, Composer, Tab completion, Cursor Rules, and advanced AI-assisted workflows
nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Claude Code
nodes.description: Introduction to Anthropic's CLI-based AI coding agent — terminal-native, agentic, and built for real engineering workflows
nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Working with Databases
nodes.description: Store and query structured data with SQL and SQLite — the foundation for building data-driven AI applications.
nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Web Scraping for AI
nodes.description: Collect training data and build knowledge bases by scraping the web responsibly — with BeautifulSoup, ethical guidelines, and practical patterns.
nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: RAG Fundamentals
nodes.description: Build retrieval-augmented generation pipelines from scratch — load documents, chunk text, create embeddings, store vectors, and generate grounded answers

resources.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
resources.note: The original RAG paper from Meta — worth skimming the abstract and intro to see where the idea came from.

resources.title: ChromaDB Getting Started
resources.note: The vector database used in this lesson. Their quickstart is excellent and mirrors what we build here.

resources.title: Simon Willison: RAG Is More Than Embeddings
resources.note: Practical perspective on when embeddings-based RAG works and when simpler approaches are better.

resources.title: Systematically Improving Your RAG
resources.note: Jason Liu's structured approach to RAG improvement — goes beyond 'add more chunks' to systematic diagnosis and iteration.

resources.title: Building LLM Applications for Production
resources.note: Chip Huyen covers production considerations for LLM apps including RAG — latency, cost, evaluation, and the messy realities of shipping.

nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Prompt Engineering
nodes.description: Go beyond tips and tricks to engineer reliable, testable prompts that produce consistent results across system prompts, few-shot patterns, structured output, and chain-of-thought reasoning

resources.title: Prompt Engineering
resources.note: Lilian Weng's comprehensive taxonomy of prompting techniques — covers zero-shot, few-shot, CoT, and more with clear examples and paper references.

resources.title: Prompt Injection
resources.note: Simon Willison's ongoing series on prompt injection attacks — essential reading on the security implications of deploying LLM-powered applications.

nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: How AI APIs Work
nodes.description: Understand the anatomy of AI API calls, make your first requests to Claude and OpenAI, and learn to manage keys, tokens, and costs like a professional

resources.title: llm: A CLI utility for interacting with Large Language Models
resources.note: Simon Willison's CLI tool for experimenting with multiple AI APIs from the terminal — great for quickly testing prompts across providers.

resources.title: Let's build GPT: from scratch, in code
resources.note: Andrej Karpathy builds a transformer from scratch — understanding what's behind the API makes you a far better consumer of it.

nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Evaluation and Observability
nodes.description: The article most AI tutorials skip — how to systematically test your AI applications, build eval suites, track costs, and monitor production behavior

resources.title: Your AI Product Needs Evals
resources.note: Hamel Husain's practical eval framework — the single best starting point for teams that know they need evals but don't know where to begin.

resources.title: Building LLM Applications for Production
resources.note: Chip Huyen's systematic approach to LLM evaluation — covers metrics, failure modes, and the evaluation pipeline from prototype to production.

resources.title: Instructor: Structured Outputs for LLMs
resources.note: Jason Liu's library for structured LLM outputs with Pydantic validation — invaluable for building reliable, type-safe evaluation pipelines.

nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Build an Agent
nodes.description: Capstone project — build a personal assistant agent from scratch with file I/O, code execution, web search, and proper error handling

resources.title: Build an Agent
resources.note: LangChain's official agent tutorial — useful to compare a framework-based approach against the from-scratch approach in this lesson.

resources.title: Creating a LLM-as-Judge That Drives Real Improvements
resources.note: Hamel Husain on evaluating agent outputs with LLM judges — critical for knowing whether your agent is actually getting better.

nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Build a Chatbot
nodes.description: Build a real conversational AI application with multi-turn memory, streaming responses, and a web interface you can share with anyone
nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: AI-Powered Data Analysis
nodes.description: Combine pandas, visualization libraries, and AI to analyze datasets faster — from exploratory analysis to automated insight generation.

resources.title: Wes McKinney: Python for Data Analysis

nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Agents and Tool Use
nodes.description: Understand AI agents that take actions in the world — define tools as JSON schemas, let the model decide when to call them, and build your first agentic loop

resources.title: LLM Powered Autonomous Agents
resources.note: The definitive survey of agent architectures — covers planning, memory, and tool use with clear diagrams and references to key papers.

resources.title: LangGraph Documentation
resources.note: Framework for building stateful, multi-step agent workflows as graphs — useful reference even if you build your own agent loop.

resources.title: State of GPT
resources.note: Andrej Karpathy explains how LLMs work under the hood — understanding the training pipeline helps you build better agents.

nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: What Is Programming? Code, Languages, and How Computers Think
nodes.description: Understand what code actually is, why programming languages exist, why Python is the go-to for AI, and what it means to run a program
nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: What Is AI? The Real Story
nodes.description: Understand what artificial intelligence actually is, how it's used today, and why applied AI engineering is one of the most exciting career paths you can choose

resources.title: State of AI Report 2025
resources.note: Annual overview of where AI capabilities actually stand — good reality check against the hype cycle.

resources.title: Andrej Karpathy: Intro to Large Language Models
resources.note: The best single video for understanding what LLMs actually are, from the former head of AI at Tesla.

resources.title: Anthropic Research Index
resources.note: If you want to see what frontier AI research looks like from a safety-focused lab, start here.

resources.title: Why 2026 Belongs to the 'Applied AI Engineer'
resources.note: Gary Sheng and Travis Oliphant make the case for 'applied AI engineer' as a distinct role — the framing that inspired this entire learning tree.

nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Virtual Environments and Dependencies
nodes.description: Learn to isolate Python projects with virtual environments and manage packages — essential skills before building anything real.
nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Setting Up Cursor: Your AI-Powered Workspace
nodes.description: Download, install, and configure Cursor — the AI-native code editor that will be your primary tool throughout this curriculum
nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Reading and Writing Data
nodes.description: Work with files, CSV, JSON, and live APIs in Python — the skills you need before connecting to AI services
nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Python Through AI: Learn by Reading, Not Memorizing
nodes.description: Learn Python fundamentals the modern way — give AI a task, read what it writes, understand the patterns, and modify the code yourself

resources.title: Practical Deep Learning for Coders
resources.note: Jeremy Howard's famous course teaches deep learning by doing — the same learn-by-building philosophy we use here, scaled up to neural networks.

resources.title: NumPy: The Absolute Beginner's Guide
resources.note: NumPy is the foundation of Python's entire data ecosystem. Understanding arrays here pays off in every AI library you'll touch.

nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Navigating an IDE: Finding Your Way Around Cursor
nodes.description: Learn the layout of your code editor — the file explorer, editor area, terminal, and essential keyboard shortcuts that make you fast
nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Git Basics: Saving Snapshots of Your Work
nodes.description: Learn version control with Git and GitHub — track changes, write meaningful commits, and push your code to the cloud

resources.title: CodeSearchNet
resources.note: GitHub's ML project for understanding code at scale — a concrete example of how git-hosted code collaboration fuels AI research.

nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Your First Conversation with AI in Cursor
nodes.description: Learn to use Cursor's AI features — chat, inline editing, and the composer — to write code by having a conversation instead of typing it from memory
nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Files, Folders, and the Terminal
nodes.description: Understand how your computer organizes files, navigate with the command line, and run Python scripts from the terminal
nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Debugging with AI
nodes.description: Turn cryptic error messages into learning opportunities — use AI to understand, diagnose, and fix bugs in your code.
nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Build Your First Script
nodes.description: Combine everything you've learned into a real, useful Python project — planned with AI, built step by step, and pushed to GitHub
nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: APIs and Web Basics
nodes.description: Understand how applications talk to each other over the web — HTTP requests, JSON data, and the basics of APIs that power modern software.
nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Structured Outputs & Data Extraction
nodes.description: Learn to extract structured, validated data from LLMs using tools like Instructor and Pydantic — the backbone of reliable AI-powered data pipelines.

resources.title: Instructor Documentation
resources.note: Jason Liu's Instructor library — the go-to tool for extracting structured data from LLMs with Pydantic validation.

resources.title: Systematically Improving Your RAG
resources.note: Jason Liu's structured approach to RAG improvement, with heavy use of structured outputs for evaluation and extraction.

resources.title: Building LLM Applications for Production
resources.note: Chip Huyen covers production considerations for LLM apps — structured outputs are a recurring theme for reliability.

nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Multi-Modal AI
nodes.description: Go beyond text — work with images, audio, and video through multi-modal AI APIs to build applications that see, hear, and understand.

resources.title: Andrej Karpathy: Let's build the GPT Tokenizer

nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: ML Systems Design
nodes.description: Design reliable AI systems for production — covering architecture patterns, monitoring, deployment strategies, and the operational practices that separate prototypes from products.

resources.title: Designing Machine Learning Systems
resources.note: Chip Huyen's book and blog cover the full lifecycle of ML systems — from data pipelines to deployment to monitoring.

resources.title: Machine Learning Systems Design
resources.note: Chip's open-source repo with frameworks for thinking about ML systems design problems — great interview prep too.

resources.title: Hamel Husain's Blog
resources.note: Practical ML engineering perspectives from someone who has shipped production ML systems at scale.

nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: LLM Security & Safety
nodes.description: Learn to identify and defend against prompt injection, jailbreaks, and data exfiltration attacks — essential knowledge for deploying LLMs in production.

resources.title: Simon Willison: Prompt Injection Series
resources.note: The definitive collection of writing on prompt injection — Simon has been tracking and documenting this class of vulnerability since the beginning.

resources.title: Adversarial Attacks on LLMs
resources.note: Lilian Weng's thorough survey of adversarial attack techniques and defenses for large language models.

nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Fine-Tuning Fundamentals
nodes.description: Understand when and how to fine-tune language models — from dataset preparation to LoRA adapters to evaluation — and when simpler approaches are better.

resources.title: Practical Deep Learning for Coders
resources.note: Jeremy Howard's legendary course — the fine-tuning sections are especially relevant and remarkably accessible.

resources.title: Hamel Husain's Blog
resources.note: Hamel writes extensively about practical fine-tuning workflows, dataset curation, and evaluation — essential reading.

resources.title: Designing Machine Learning Systems
resources.note: Chip Huyen's work on ML systems design provides crucial context for when fine-tuning fits into the broader system.

nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Deploying AI Applications
nodes.description: Ship your AI projects to the world — from FastAPI backends to Docker containers to cloud deployment, with cost management and scaling strategies.
nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network

nodes.title: Advanced RAG Patterns
nodes.description: Go beyond basic RAG with hybrid search, intelligent chunking strategies, reranking, and evaluation-driven iteration to build production-quality retrieval systems.

resources.title: Simon Willison: RAG Is More Than Embeddings
resources.note: Practical perspective on when embeddings-based RAG works, when it doesn't, and what alternatives exist.

resources.title: Systematically Improving Your RAG
resources.note: Jason Liu's structured approach to diagnosing and improving RAG pipelines — goes well beyond 'add more chunks.'

nodes.author: ALL Applied AI Network
